name: stamped-from-the-beginning
description: Ibram X. Kendi's 'Stamped from the Beginning: The Definitive History of Racist Ideas in America' — a sweeping history arguing that racist ideas were created to justify discriminatory policies.
useCase: Understand the history of racism in America, learn the three camps framework, and apply antiracist thinking to contemporary issues.
knownRisks: Challenging content — directly confronts deeply held beliefs about American history and equality
Academic in scope — dense reading, the skill distills key arguments
Some may find Kendi's framework reductive — the three-camp model simplifies complex reality
